af_score_components.notes: HCP Terraform (formerly Terraform Cloud) REST API. No MCP server. JSON:API format with bearer tokens (organization/team/user tokens). Comprehensive documentation with detailed error responses. Rate limits documented. SOC2/GDPR/FedRAMP compliance. Cursor-based pagination. Strong enterprise-grade API.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. Bearer token auth with fine-grained scopes (organization/team/user tokens with different permission levels). FedRAMP authorization indicates rigorous security posture. SOC2 Type II certified. Sensitive data managed via workspace variables with encryption. Strong overall security.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: HashiCorp maintains a status page (status.hashicorp.com). Terraform Cloud has a strong track record of stability. API versioned with documented migration guides. JSON:API format provides consistent error structures. Enterprise SLA available. Good retry guidance in docs.

af_score_components.notes: Rust-based Terraform MCP server exposing 31 tools for Terraform operations. Has MCP quality 78. Requires local Terraform CLI installation. Dangerous operations (apply, destroy) disabled by default for safety. Good tool coverage: plan, validate, workspace management, state inspection. auth_complexity=100 as it uses local CLI credentials.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for Terraform Cloud API calls. Inherits Terraform CLI credential management (tokens stored in ~/.terraform.d/credentials.tfrc.json). Dangerous ops disabled by default is a good security choice. Rust's memory safety reduces vulnerability risk. No known CVEs in recent releases.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local MCP server — reliability depends on local Terraform CLI setup. No SLA as it's a community tool. Semantic versioning used. Breaking changes tracked in changelog. Error propagation from Terraform CLI is generally clear. Rust implementation provides process stability.

af_score_components.notes: TiDB Cloud Data API. No MCP server. HTTP Digest authentication is unusual and adds complexity for agents — most SDKs support it but it's non-standard. MySQL-compatible HTAP distributed database. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliant. JSON responses. auth_complexity=55 for non-standard Digest auth that requires nonce handling.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. HTTP Digest auth provides challenge-response security but is uncommon in modern APIs. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. GDPR compliant. TiDB Cloud manages infrastructure security. API keys scoped per project. Data encryption at rest and in transit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: TiDB Cloud has status page and SLA documentation. PingCAP maintains enterprise reliability standards. API versioned. Multi-region deployment supports high availability. Error responses follow consistent structure. Retry guidance available in docs.

af_score_components.notes: Todoist task management API. Has MCP quality 82 (official MCP server). Dual API: REST v2 for CRUD and Sync API v9 for bulk operations. OAuth2 and personal API tokens supported. Rate limits documented. Good error handling. Strong MCP integration makes it very agent-friendly.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes (task:add, data:read, data:delete, project:delete). Personal API tokens for simpler access. GDPR compliant. Token revocation supported. Access tokens never logged. Doist maintains security practices consistent with enterprise requirements.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Todoist status page available. API v2 has been stable for years. Versioned APIs with deprecation notices. Sync API provides conflict resolution. Error responses well-structured with codes. Idempotent operations supported. Good track record of backward compatibility.

af_score_components.notes: Together AI LLM inference platform. No MCP server. OpenAI-compatible API — drop-in replacement enables immediate agent adoption. Bearer token auth (simplest). Comprehensive documentation covering completions, embeddings, images, reranking, and fine-tuning. SOC2 certified. Rate limits clearly documented with per-model limits.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. API key via Authorization header (never in URL). SOC2 Type II certified. Enterprise security controls. Keys scoped per organization. Token rotation supported. No data training on user inputs by default. Security-focused for enterprise AI workloads.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Together AI has status page and uptime SLA. OpenAI-compatible API ensures stability through broad ecosystem compatibility. Well-maintained SDK. Error responses follow OpenAI format (consistent). Streaming and batch inference supported. Enterprise reliability tier available.

af_score_components.notes: Together AI (shorter/alternative entry). No MCP server. Bearer token auth. OpenAI-compatible. This appears to be a less detailed entry for the same Together AI platform as together-ai-api. Good overall agent-friendliness via OpenAI compatibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. Bearer token auth via Authorization header. SOC2 certified. Security practices consistent with together-ai-api entry. No credentials in URLs. Enterprise security standards maintained.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. OpenAI-compatible API stability. Versioned endpoints. Standard error handling patterns. Reliable for inference workloads. Same platform as together-ai-api.

af_score_components.notes: Toggl time tracking API. No MCP server. HTTP Basic Auth with API token (token as username, 'api_token' as password) — slightly non-standard but simple. Two API versions: Reports API v8 and v9. JSON responses. GDPR compliant. Good documentation for core CRUD operations. auth_complexity=85 for Basic Auth with API token pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS only enforced. HTTP Basic Auth with API token is functional but less secure than Bearer tokens (base64 encoding, not encryption). Limited scope granularity — API token has broad access. GDPR compliant with data export/deletion. Toggl is a mature company with reasonable security practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Toggl Track has status page. API has been stable for years. V8 and V9 versions maintained in parallel showing good deprecation practices. Standard HTTP error codes. Rate limiting documented. Reliable for time-tracking use cases.

af_score_components.notes: TomTom mapping, routing, and geolocation APIs. No MCP server. API key as query parameter (security concern but simple). Comprehensive documentation for maps, routing, geocoding, traffic. ISO27001/GDPR compliant. Good coverage of geospatial operations. auth_complexity=85 for API key simplicity despite URL exposure risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced but API key in query parameter is a security concern — keys appear in server logs, browser history, and referrer headers. No fine-grained scopes — single API key has broad access. ISO27001 certified at infrastructure level. Mitigation: IP allowlisting and referrer restrictions available. Recommend moving to header-based auth. secret_handling=55 due to key-in-URL pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: TomTom has status page and enterprise SLAs. APIs versioned with long support windows. Global CDN for map tiles. Standard HTTP error codes with detailed messages. Retry guidance in docs. Enterprise reliability suitable for navigation/fleet applications.

af_score_components.notes: TradingView MCP server. Has MCP quality 60. No authentication required (read-only market data access). Community project, not official TradingView. Limited documentation. Provides technical analysis and charting data. auth_complexity=100 for no auth required. Lower scores reflect community project status and limited reliability guarantees.

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — read-only access to public market data. HTTPS used for upstream TradingView requests. Community project with limited security review. No formal CVE process. auth_strength=40 due to no auth (though data is public). Not suitable for production trading systems without additional security layers. Dependency hygiene uncertain as community project.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No SLA — community project dependent on TradingView's unofficial data access. Risk of breaking changes if TradingView modifies underlying APIs. No formal changelog. Error handling basic. Not recommended for production financial applications. Suitable for personal/research use only.

af_score_components.notes: Trello kanban board API. No MCP server. API key + token as query parameters (security concern). OAuth 1.0a for user authorization to get token. SOC2/GDPR/ISO27001 compliant (part of Atlassian). Well-documented REST API. Rate limits documented. auth_complexity=70 for OAuth 1.0a + query param pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced but API key and token as query parameters is a significant security concern — credentials visible in server logs and URLs. OAuth 1.0a is outdated (OAuth 2.0 preferred). SOC2/ISO27001 at infrastructure level via Atlassian. Limited scope granularity. Atlassian has announced migration to newer auth. secret_handling=60 due to credentials-in-URL pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Atlassian status page covers Trello. Enterprise SLA as part of Atlassian suite. API has been stable with long deprecation cycles. Standard HTTP error codes. Rate limit errors well-communicated. Atlassian's infrastructure investment supports strong reliability.
